basic_info.about: Established in 1636, Harvard is the oldest institution of higher education in the United States. It is a private Ivy League research university with a global reputation for academic excellence, extensive research output, and a highly selective admissions process.
basic_info.summary: Harvard University is a leading global institution comprising several undergraduate and graduate schools, including the prestigious Harvard College, Harvard Business School, and Harvard Medical School. It consistently ranks among the top universities worldwide.

accreditations.body: New England Commission of Higher Education (NECHE)

basic_info.recognition: Member of the Association of American Universities (AAU)

programs.raw_output:
Harvard University offers a comprehensive range of academic programs across 12 degree\-granting schools and the Harvard Radcliffe Institute. The university provides 50 undergraduate concentrations, over 130 graduate degrees, and numerous PhD programs. [15] [1] [2] [3] [35]

### Undergraduate Programs \(Harvard College\)

Harvard refers to its majors as "concentrations." Students typically earn a Bachelor of Arts \(A.B.\) or Bachelor of Science \(S.B.\). [13] [4]

- Social Sciences: Economics, Government, Anthropology, Sociology, and Psychology.
- Sciences & Math: Applied Mathematics, Astrophysics, Chemical and Physical Biology, Computer Science, and Statistics.
- Engineering: Bioengineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Environmental Science and Engineering.
- Arts & Humanities: Classics, Comparative Literature, History of Art and Architecture, Music, and Philosophy.
- Languages & Culture: East Asian Studies, Near Eastern Languages and Civilizations, and Romance Languages and Literatures.

### Postgraduate \(Master's\) Programs

Graduate programs are offered through specialized professional schools and the [Kenneth C. Griffin Graduate School of Arts and Sciences](https://gsas.harvard.edu/programs) \(GSAS\). [1]

- Business \(HBS\): [Master of Business Administration \(MBA\)](https://www.hbs.edu/).
- Law \(HLS\): Master of Laws \(LL.M.\).
- Public Policy \(HKS\): Master in Public Policy \(MPP\) and Master in Public Administration \(MPA\).
- Education \(HGSE\): [Master of Education \(Ed.M.\)](https://www.gse.harvard.edu/degrees/phd) with concentrations in Education Policy and Analysis, or Learning Design, Innovation, and Technology.
- Public Health \(HSPH\): Master of Public Health \(MPH\) and Master of Science \(SM\) in fields like Epidemiology.
- Design \(GSD\): Master in Architecture \(M.Arch\), Landscape Architecture, and Urban Planning.
- Engineering & Sciences \(SEAS/GSAS\): Master of Science \(SM\) in Data Science, Computational Science and Engineering, and Master of Engineering \(ME\).

### PhD and Doctoral Programs

Most PhD programs are administered through the Griffin GSAS in collaboration with other schools. [0] [7] [36]

- Arts & Humanities: PhDs in African and African American Studies, American Studies, Film and Visual Studies, and History.
- Natural Sciences: PhDs in Astronomy, Biology, Chemistry, Physics, and Earth and Planetary Sciences.
- Life Sciences \(HILS\): [PhDs in Bioinformatics](https://hms.harvard.edu/education-admissions/phd-degree-programs), Immunology, Neuroscience, and Virology.
- Social Sciences: PhDs in Economics, Political Science, Psychology, and Sociology.
- Professional Doctorates:
  - Medicine: Doctor of Medicine \(MD\) and Doctor of Dental Medicine \(DMD\).
  - Education: [Doctor of Education Leadership \(Ed.L.D.\)](https://www.gse.harvard.edu/doctorate) and PhD in Education.
  - Business: PhD programs in Accounting & Management, Marketing, and Strategy.

Are you interested in admissions requirements or application deadlines for a specific level of study?

placements.graduate_outcomes_note: 2026 data is projected based on early career outcomes and historical HBS and FAS survey trends. Average compensation includes base salary and projected bonuses.

placements.placement_highlights: Harvard University continues to see dominant outcomes in Finance, Consulting, and Technology, with over 70% of graduates in these sectors earning six-figure starting salaries. Despite a slight cooling in tech hiring, the median base salary for HBS graduates remains robust at $184,500, supported by significant signing bonuses averageing $30,000.

fees.fees_note: Undergraduate fees for the 2026-27 academic year include a base tuition of $62,226 with a total billed cost of approximately $91,634. Graduate (GSAS) and PhD students pay full tuition for the first two years, followed by significantly reduced rates for subsequent years. PhD students typically receive full funding covering all tuition, fees, and a living stipend of at least $50,000 to $56,392.

scholarships_detail.name: Harvard College Financial Aid
scholarships_detail.eligibility: Families with income below $100,000 pay nothing; up to $200,000 pay 0-10% of income.

scholarships_detail.name: PhD Funding Package
scholarships_detail.eligibility: All admitted PhD students in good academic standing.

infrastructure.details: Central hub featuring the Visitor Center, food venues, and collaborative spaces.
State-of-the-art 544,000-square-foot facility in Allston for SEAS research and teaching.
High-end instrumentation and shared research resources across the Science Division.
Includes Fogg, Busch-Reisinger, and Arthur M. Sackler Museums.

scholarships.name: Harvard College Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Undergraduate students with demonstrated financial need; covers up to full tuition and board.

scholarships.name: Harvard Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Incoming students with exceptional academic achievements and leadership potential.

scholarships.name: Harvard National Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Exceptional students from the United States demonstrating academic excellence.

scholarships.name: Undergraduate Scholarship for International Students
scholarships.eligibility: International undergraduate applicants with exceptional academic records and financial need.

scholarships.name: Boustany MBA Harvard Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: MBA candidates with high academic merit; includes financial aid and internship expenses.

scholarships.name: Horace W. Goldsmith Fellowship
scholarships.eligibility: First-year HBS students with leadership roles in non-profit or public sectors.
